items.description: Small single-stroke ink drawing of a long-necked creature (bird- or wyvern-like) in profile facing right, with rounded head, curved neck, beak/snout, compact body, and raised wing- or tail-like extension; placed centrally in the blank space below the final text line.
items.scholarly_note: Spontaneous marginal doodle or pen trial by the scribe or a near-contemporary hand. Minimalist zoomorphic sketches of this type occur in 13th-century Icelandic Gothic manuscripts and usually bear no direct relation to the surrounding heroic text (Guðrúnarkviða I).

items.description: Enlarged capital 'S' in the same dark ink, positioned at the left edge of the text block and protruding slightly into the margin area.
items.scholarly_note: Scribal initial marking the opening of a stanza or narrative unit. Characteristic of the Codex Regius main hand's method of textual articulation in the Poetic Edda.

items.description: Small circular dark spot or puncture near the top center of the leaf, above the first lines of text.
items.scholarly_note: Natural parchment defect, possible insect bore, or later puncture; does not intersect or obscure text.

items.description: Extensive mottled brown discoloration and surface staining across the lower third of the page, extending upward into the text area and left margin.
items.scholarly_note: Typical medieval vellum age staining and handling marks; iron-gall ink remains legible. May reflect greater exposure or use of this portion of the leaf.

items.description: Darkened and abraded staining concentrated along the left edge and adjacent to the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Edge staining from prolonged handling, rubbing, and environmental exposure over centuries.

items.description: Thin curved ink line or tail descending from near the end of the final text line, extending rightward past the doodle.
items.scholarly_note: Possible scribal flourish, continuation of the final stroke, or accidental pen mark; not part of the main text.

scratches: Faint linear abrasions and surface wear visible within the stained lower and left regions, where the parchment nap has been disturbed or lightly scratched.
Minor hairline marks consistent with vellum preparation or later handling abrasion.
damage_notes: Heavily abraded and irregularly torn bottom edge with substantial loss of original vellum material.
Worn, chipped, and irregular left margin contour.
General thinning, cockling, and edge wear of the parchment sheet, most severe at the foot.
Small circular defect near top center.
Localized surface loss and discoloration in lower half, typical of a frequently consulted leaf in the codex.
summary: Page 95 of Codex Regius (Guðrúnarkviða I) contains a densely written text block in the main scribe's Gothic book hand with narrow margins. The primary non-textual feature is a small spontaneous doodle of a long-necked bird- or dragon-like creature in the lower margin. The parchment exhibits prominent brown age staining concentrated in the lower and left areas, significant material loss and abrasion along the bottom edge, a small top defect, and surface scratches. An enlarged initial 'S' marks a textual division. No interlinear additions, major scribal corrections, faces, additional animals, offset ink, or marginal writing are clearly visible at the provided image resolution. The surface condition is consistent with a well-used 13th-century Icelandic vellum manuscript.
